decks.notes: The main focus of this Arclight Phoenix aggro deck is to rapidly cycle through cards, play cheap spells, and utilize the graveyard mechanic to consistently bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard. By utilizing cards like Vibrant Outburst, Traumatic Critique, and Picklock Prankster to fill the graveyard, combined with spells like Consider, Academic Dispute, and Fiery Impulse to fuel the Phoenix's revival, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a quick and aggressive playstyle. Additionally, cards like Thing in the Ice provide additional threats to the opponent while Treasure Cruise helps refill the hand for more explosive plays.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with Arclight Phoenixes and then returning them to the battlefield for aggressive play. It utilizes cheap spells like Fiery Impulse and Lightning Axe to trigger the Phoenix's ability, while also including card draw with Opt and Treasure Cruise to find key pieces. The inclusion of Picklock Prankster and Ledger Shredder helps further enable graveyard shenanigans, while having a few disruptive elements like Spell Pierce and Brazen Borrower to control the game. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, recurring threats and efficient spells.

This Pioneer deck focuses on quickly bringing back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard to deal damage and win the game. The deck includes cards like Consider, Opt, and Sleight of Hand to help dig for Arclight Phoenix and other key cards. Lightning Axe and Fiery Impulse provide removal options, while Picklock Prankster and Ledger Shredder help with card advantage. The deck also includes some counter spells like Spell Pierce for protection. Overall, the deck aims to be fast-paced and efficient in bringing back Arclight Phoenix for repeated damage.

This Golgari Aggro deck focuses on efficiently deploying creatures and utilizing mana acceleration to overwhelm opponents quickly. The deck features a mix of powerful creatures like Tyvar, Jubilant Brawler and Craterhoof Behemoth, as well as mana dorks like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves. The main strategy is to establish a board presence early, pump up creatures with cards like Nature's Rhythm, and finish off opponents with a big swing. The deck also includes some toolbox creatures like Fiend Artisan for added versatility.

The focus of this deck is to consistently get Arclight Phoenix into play and utilize spells to trigger its ability to return from the graveyard. The deck runs cheap spells to help fill the graveyard and control the board early on, while also drawing cards to maintain card advantage. Ledger Shredder and Picklock Prankster provide additional synergy with the discard effects, and the inclusion of a few utility spells like Torch the Tower and Into the Flood Maw offer flexibility in dealing with threats. With careful card selection and sequencing, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a flurry of fiery threats.

This deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient creatures like Badgermole Cub and Archon of Emeria, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with aggression and tempo plays, all while maintaining card advantage with cards like Collected Company. With a mix of powerful creatures and disruptive spells, the deck aims to outpace the opponent and secure victory through a combination of creature damage and burn spells from Elspeth, Storm Slayer.

The deck focuses on using a combination of efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, along with hand disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Duress, to control the opponent's board and hand. It also utilizes powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Emeritus of Abundance to apply pressure and keep the opponent on the back foot. The mana base is designed to support both green and black spells, with utility lands like Mutavault and Boseiju, Who Endures to provide additional options. The overall strategy is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing a strong board presence.

The main concept of this deck is to use aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The deck also utilizes cards like Academic Dispute and Stormchaser's Talent to draw more cards and maintain pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Secret Identity and Slickshot Show-Off allows for additional versatility in combat situations, making it difficult for the opponent to predict your moves. Overall, the deck focuses on fast-paced aggression and card advantage to overwhelm the opponent.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by using counterspells, removal, and card draw to outvalue the opponent. The key strategy is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up powerful win conditions such as Planeswalkers and powerful sorceries. By managing the battlefield and maintaining card advantage, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and secure victory through incremental advantage. It utilizes a mix of counterspells, removal, card draw, and powerful threats to control the game and emerge victorious in the late game.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells and removal while generating card advantage with Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Memory Deluge. The deck also aims to use Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion to provide additional value by blinking permanents. The inclusion of cards like The Wandering Emperor and Erode allows the deck to handle a variety of threats while maintaining a stable board state. Overall, the strategy revolves around outlasting opponents and gradually taking control of the game through efficient removal and powerful card draw spells.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruption. With cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, the deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan while deploying threats like Skyclave Apparition and Elspeth, Storm Slayer to apply pressure. Collected Company allows for explosive creature plays, while cards like Enduring Innocence and Ouroboroid provide additional resilience. By combining efficient creatures with disruption elements, this deck looks to outpace and outmaneuver opponents in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

This Control deck focuses on utilizing Niv-Mizzet Reborn's ability to draw cards to gain card advantage and control the game. It includes a range of powerful multicolored cards that can be searched for with Bring to Light, such as removal spells, creatures, and additional copies of Niv-Mizzet Reborn. The deck also has a companion in Yorion, Sky Nomad for additional value and flexibility. With a mix of mana fixing, removal, and threats, this deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents in a long game.

The deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, utilizing cards like Parhelion II, Greasefang, and Cache Grab to generate value and overwhelm the opponent. It includes a mix of removal spells, mana sources, and card draw to control the game while setting up the combo. The deck aims to ramp up quickly and disrupt the opponent's strategy while building towards a powerful board presence that can quickly close out the game.

The key strategy of this Selesnya Aggro deck in Pioneer 1 vs 1 format is to quickly flood the board with low-cost creatures like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves, then use Collected Company to bring more creatures onto the battlefield. Cards like Giada, Font of Hope, Skyclave Apparition, and Resplendent Angel provide additional value and threats to the opponent. The deck combines aggressive creatures with life gain and angel synergies to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory through a combination of damage and board presence.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful control elements such as removal spells and hand disruption to disrupt the opponent's game plan while steadily building up resources and eventually overwhelming them with powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck also includes utility lands to provide additional versatility and resilience. The strategy is to maintain control of the game through disruption and removal until the opponent is unable to recover, leading to a victory through attrition and overwhelming board presence.

This aggro deck focuses on fast mana acceleration and creature aggression to overwhelm the opponent quickly. Using efficient creatures like Collected Company and Skyclave Apparition, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain board control. The inclusion of disruptive creatures like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Aven Interrupter helps to disrupt the opponent's game plan while boosting the deck's tempo. With a mix of powerful creatures and disruption, this Selesnya Aggro deck aims to establish dominance on the battlefield and secure a swift victory.
